compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is in a state of advanced decay. The registered specimens (P1-P4) are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor remains, but the plant is absent.

plant_audit.P1: Empty pot; soil appears dry and undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P4: Empty pot; no evidence of growth.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Uniformly dark, dry, and devoid of organic activity.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white, crystalline fragments (likely perlite or mineral salts) in the foreground pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green sprout in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: The green sprout exhibits signs of chlorosis and postural collapse, eventually disappearing.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Total loss of all biological material. The biome is now effectively sterile.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: Total Biome Collapse. The lack of light and potential environmental stressors have led to the complete senescence and disappearance of all specimens.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Zero green biomass detected in the current frame.

anomalies: Crystalline debris in the foreground pot.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, which contained a single, fragile sprout, the biome has regressed into a state of total dormancy/death. By the current timestamp, no living plant matter remains in any of the pots. The white rabbit anchor serves only to highlight the emptiness of the P3 container. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
